# overflow-aware-list

> A monorepo of packages that render only the items that physically fit inside a container — measured with ResizeObserver — and pass the overflow to a render prop/snippet for a "+N" badge, ellipsis, or any custom indicator. Zero dependencies. Works horizontally and vertically, and supports slicing from either end of the list.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rai-aashish/overflow-aware-list
- React demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/react/
- Svelte demo: https://rai-aashish.github.io/overflow-aware-list/svelte

## Packages

| Package | npm | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `@overflow-aware-list/core` | `npm install @overflow-aware-list/core` | Framework-agnostic controller + pure computation functions |
| `@overflow-aware-list/react` | `npm install @overflow-aware-list/react` | React 18/19 component |
| `@overflow-aware-list/svelte` | `npm install @overflow-aware-list/svelte` | Svelte 5 component |

## Detailed docs per package

- [packages/core/llms.txt](packages/core/llms.txt) — types, `OverflowListController` API, pure functions, custom integration guide
- [packages/react/llms.txt](packages/react/llms.txt) — `<OverflowList>` props, usage examples, styling, implementation notes
- [packages/svelte/llms.txt](packages/svelte/llms.txt) — `<OverflowList>` props, snippet API, usage examples, styling, Svelte 5 notes

## Core concept

All packages share the same two-render-pass design:

1. **Visible container** — `overflow: hidden`, shows only the items that fit (`visibleItems`).
2. **Off-screen measurement layer** — `position: absolute; visibility: hidden; left: -9999px` — renders *all* items so `ResizeObserver` can measure their natural sizes.

The `OverflowListController` (in `core`) reads sizes from the off-screen layer and calls `onStateChange` with:

```ts
{
  visibleItems: T[];   // items to render in the visible container
  hiddenItems: T[];    // items to pass to the overflow indicator
  isOverflowing: boolean;
  startIndex: number;  // index into original array where visible slice starts
  measured: boolean;   // false until first measurement completes
}
```

Framework wrappers are thin shells around this controller.

## sliceFrom

- `"start"` (default) — keep first N items, overflow indicator at **end**. Good for tag clouds, nav links.
- `"end"` — keep last N items, overflow indicator at **start**. Good for breadcrumbs, file paths.

## Quick start — React

```tsx
import { OverflowList } from "@overflow-aware-list/react";

<OverflowList
  items={tags}
  renderItem={(tag) => <span className="tag">{tag}</span>}
  renderMore={(hidden) => <span>+{hidden.length}</span>}
/>
```

## Quick start — Svelte 5

```svelte
<script>
  import { OverflowList } from "@overflow-aware-list/svelte";
</script>

<OverflowList items={tags}>
  {#snippet renderItem(tag)}<span class="tag">{tag}</span>{/snippet}
  {#snippet renderMore(hidden)}<span>+{hidden.length}</span>{/snippet}
</OverflowList>
```

## Quick start — custom framework (using core directly)

```ts
import { OverflowListController } from "@overflow-aware-list/core";

const controller = new OverflowListController({
  items,
  direction: "horizontal", // or "vertical"
  sliceFrom: "start",       // or "end"
  onStateChange: (state) => render(state),
});

controller.mountContainer(containerEl);
items.forEach((_, i) => controller.registerItemElement(i, offScreenItemEls[i]));
controller.registerMoreElement(offScreenMoreEl);

// on prop change:
controller.updateOptions({ items: newItems });

// on unmount:
controller.destroy();
```

## Monorepo structure

```
packages/
  core/     @overflow-aware-list/core    — framework-agnostic
  react/    @overflow-aware-list/react   — React wrapper
  svelte/   @overflow-aware-list/svelte  — Svelte 5 wrapper
apps/
  react-demo/   — demo app (uses @overflow-aware-list/react)
  svelte-demo/  — demo app (uses @overflow-aware-list/svelte)
```

Build tool: Bun workspaces. `core` must be built before framework packages.

## SSR

All components initialise with all items visible (safe for server rendering). The client trims after the first `ResizeObserver` callback. `state.measured` is `false` until that first measurement.
